strategy.name: BTC_XAU_Bollinger_Squeeze_v3
strategy.rationale: 1m squeeze: wait for ATR compression then Bollinger band breakout with momentum. Fast resolution reduces hold time and gap risk. XAU session-gated. Higher trade frequency offsets per-trade edge.

history.analysis: 30% WR with PF 0.48 is deeply unprofitable. Mean-reversion fires against trend; stops hit 7/10 times. Time-exits salvage only small wins. Spread kills ETH; low ATR filter still allows trending moves.
mistakes: Mean-reversion entries taken against prevailing momentum - BTC longs repeatedly stopped as price continued falling (66442->66303, 66232->66104, 65505->65464)
ATR<0.15 filter too permissive - trades entered in directional moves masquerading as low-vol, not true consolidation
RSI<32 / price<SMA34 combo fires during strong downtrends, not genuine oversold bounces - no confirmation of reversal
0.35% stop vs 0.85% target gives 2.4R but actual WR of 30% requires >29.4% to break even - marginally viable but spread erodes it entirely
changes: Switch from mean-reversion to trend-following with momentum confirmation to align with actual market behavior
Tighten ATR filter to require genuine low-volatility consolidation before breakout, not just <0.15
Add session filter for XAU to avoid Friday close gaps and Monday opens
Reduce maxHoldMinutes to force quicker resolution - scalping should not hold 90 minutes
Use BTC and XAU only - ETH spread (0.16%) is 8x BTC and unviable for scalping at these sizes

strategy.name: BTC_XAU_Bollinger_Squeeze_v3
strategy.rationale: 1m squeeze: wait for ATR compression then Bollinger band breakout with momentum. Fast resolution reduces hold time and gap risk. XAU session-gated. Higher trade frequency offsets per-trade edge.

history.analysis: All 3 trades stopped out immediately; mean-reversion entries in trending/volatile conditions. SMA-above-price long logic contradictory (buying above SMA during dip). ATR filter too loose at 0.3%. TP/SL ratio 1.9:1 insufficient after spread drag.
mistakes: Contradictory long condition: requires price_vs_sma op '>' (price above SMA) while seeking oversold dip - these conflict, producing bad entries
0.2% stop is too tight for 5m BTC/XAU noise; all 3 trades stopped on normal wick before any reversion could occur
ATR filter <0.3% passed during genuinely volatile candles - threshold needs tightening to <0.15% to avoid choppy entries
No trend alignment filter - entering mean-reversion longs into downtrends; need confirmation that broader trend supports direction
changes: Fix price_vs_sma logic: longs require price BELOW SMA (true reversion), shorts require price ABOVE SMA
Widen stop to 0.35% and TP to 0.85% for 2.4R minimum, giving edge even at 40% WR
Tighten ATR filter to <0.15% to avoid high-volatility traps
Add SMA trend filter on longer period to confirm directional bias before entry

strategy.name: BTC_XAU_TrendReversion_Fixed
strategy.rationale: Corrected mean-reversion: price must be BELOW SMA for longs, RSI oversold, low ATR, with 200-SMA trend alignment. Wider 2.4R ratio survives spread at modest WR.

history.analysis: Pairs cheap-spread assets trading RSI extremes vs SMA: oversold pullbacks to SMA on low ATR for controlled reversion. ETH excluded due to 8x spread penalty; diversifies across uncorrelated assets.
changes: Tournament of 3: XAU_MeanReversion_Bollinger (5m) net $-15.19 in 4 trades | BTC_Momentum_Breakout (5m) net $-5.94 in 8 trades | BTC_XAU_DualAsset_SMAReversion (5m) net $-2.19 in 2 trades <- DEPLOYED

strategy.name: BTC_XAU_DualAsset_SMAReversion
strategy.rationale: Pairs cheap-spread assets trading RSI extremes vs SMA: oversold pullbacks to SMA on low ATR for controlled reversion. ETH excluded due to 8x spread penalty; diversifies across uncorrelated assets.
